body.title: [open_questions] test_architecture_intention_points red: an implementing_files entry is a dict, not a path string
body.intention: I intend that every architecture intention-point's pointers resolve to real files, so an intention-point always leads a reader from intent to the code that implements it.
body.description:
Pre-existing failure, confirmed at a clean worktree on the stub commit — NOT introduced by T-links-intentions-edge-kind.

`tests/test_architecture_intention_points.py::test_every_intention_point_pointer_resolves` raises `TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for /: 'PosixPath' and 'dict'` at line 45. The test does `_REPO / rel` for every entry in `implementing_files` / `reference_docs`, assuming each is a path string. At least one intention-point stores a dict instead — schema drift in the intention-point envelope.

Distinct from T-arch-points-stale-impl-paths, which is about pointers that are strings but stale. This one is about a pointer that is the wrong TYPE, so the test cannot even check whether it resolves.

**Affected files:** tests/test_architecture_intention_points.py, the offending devlab/runtime/memory/architecture/*.json record(s), whatever writer emitted a dict.
**Design rules:** canonical memory home; JSON flat-file; intention-points are intent->implementation pointers.
**Scope boundary:** IN — find the offending record(s), decide whether the dict form is intended (and if so teach the test to read it) or a writer bug (and if so fix the writer + record). OUT — the stale-path sweep (T-arch-points-stale-impl-paths); adding new intention-points.
**Test plan:** the existing node is the test. Red today (TypeError); green when every pointer resolves. A TypeError is not a verdict — the node must fail on a missing path, not on a type error, so first make it type-correct, then let it report real misses.
**Completion criteria:** test_every_intention_point_pointer_resolves passes, or fails with a genuine list of unresolved paths rather than a TypeError.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

---
## Open questions (kicked back to design 2026-07-10, cc.0 — scope entanglement found on contact)

Making the test type-correct (reading `dict.path`) does NOT yield one miss — it surfaces ~100
unresolved pointers across nearly every architecture record. They split into three distinct
classes, only one of which this ticket was scoped for. That means "teach the test to read the
dict" cannot land a green (or even a clean, actionable-list) close without deciding the questions
below, and it collides with the sibling stale-path ticket. Findings:

- The dict form ({path, anchor, status:"live"|"planned — T-…"}) is INTENTIONAL and richer than a
  bare string (it carries the in-file anchor + a live/planned marker). Only two records use it
  (feedback-edges, validity-conditions, both 2026-07-07). So the writer is not buggy — the test is
  simply string-only. That part is clear.
- Once type-correct, the misses are dominated by (a) stale pre-single-package-reorg paths
  (`bus/imap_server.py`, `diagnostic_base/base.py`, `devices/igor/…`, `skeleton/…`) — these are
  exactly T-arch-points-stale-impl-paths, explicitly OUT of scope here; and (b) `reference_docs`
  entries that are NOT file paths at all — they hold `decision:D-…` refs and prose like
  "(the decision this designs)". Resolving those as filesystem paths is a category error.

Q1: Should `reference_docs` be resolved as filesystem paths at all? Many entries are decision-IDs
    / prose, not paths. If they are refs, the test must resolve them differently (decision store)
    or exempt them — which is a contract decision about what an intention-point pointer IS.
Q2: For `implementing_files`, should a `planned` dict entry be required to resolve? A planned
    pointer names an existing FILE with a not-yet-built anchor, so the path can still resolve — but
    directory/glob/TBD forms (`devices/*/shim.py`, `config/ground_loop/`, `TBD — …`) legitimately
    don't. What forms count as "resolvable"?
Q3: This ticket vs T-arch-points-stale-impl-paths: after the type-fix, the remaining misses ARE the
    stale-path sweep. Should the two merge, or does this one close at "type-correct + emits a genuine
    list" (its stated DoD) and the sweep own the greening? If the latter, the suite carries a red
    test until the sweep lands — is that acceptable, or should the node xfail with a pointer to the
    sweep ticket?

A1:
A2:
A3:
body.completion_criteria: The node no longer raises TypeError; every pointer is a path string and either resolves or is reported as missing.
